title: rosa canina var. rubescens
intro: Rosa canina var. rubescens, also known as the dwarf dog rose or sweet-briar rose, is a variety of the dog rose plant, which is a type of wild rose native to Europe and western Asia. This shrub is known for its medicinal properties, including its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. rubescens include its ability to aid in the treatment of diarrhea, sore throats, and wounds, as well as its potential to improve cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of certain infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to make rosehip tea, a popular remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
